title: Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS)
text:
Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS) - SupportGoWhere
Supplementary Retirement Scheme (SRS)
Share link
Provides tax relief to encourage individuals to save for retirement, over and above their CPF savings. Investment returns are tax-free before withdrawal and only 50% of withdrawals are taxable at retirement.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
50% tax concession on withdrawals from SRS at retirement
Tax relief on SRS contributions
Collapse all sections
About this support

Provides equivalent tax relief on contributions to SRS, capped at $15,300 for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents and $35,700 for foreigners

Investment returns on SRS contributions are tax-free before withdrawal, and only 50% of withdrawals will be taxable at retirement

Who is this for?

You can only have 1 SRS account at any point in time

To open an SRS account, you must:

be at least 18 years old.

not be an undischarged bankrupt.

have the mental capacity to make own decisions

not have an existing SRS account (including suspended accounts) with the same or another operator

not have a pending application with another SRS operator.

not have a previous SRS account with all its funds withdrawn, on medical grounds or after reaching the prevailing retirement age (at time of your first contribution)

To qualify for SRS Tax Relief:

You must be a tax resident for the Year of Assessment following the year of SRS contribution

Your SRS account was not suspended on 31 Dec of the year of contribution

You did not withdraw, from your SRS account, the same amount as you contributed in the same year of contribution

Please note that the following conditions / criteria apply to SRS withdrawals:

SRS withdrawals can be made anytime. Withdrawals can be made in cash or in the form of investments

Withdrawals are subject to income tax (based on the time and circumstances of withdrawal) and will be considered part of your taxable income

Foreigners and Permanent Residents who make withdrawals are subject to withholding tax

Withdrawals made before retirement age will be subject to penalties unless they were made due to exceptional circumstances.



You may read up on the additional criteria and details on SRS withdrawals and the relevant taxes here.

How to apply?

To open an SRS account:

Choose 1 of 3 SRS operators (DBS Group Holdings Ltd, Overseas-Chinese Banking Corporation (OCBC) Ltd, United Overseas Bank (UOB) Ltd.) to set up an SRS account with

Tax relief will be provided automatically, if eligible

For foreigners:

You must complete the SRS Declaration Form


Note: You must update your SRS bank operator if you become a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, even if you have already contributed this year, as your maximum contribution amount will have to be recalculated and may be pro-rated.

Where can I find help?

View more details

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
